items.title: As Tim Cook steps down, Apple hit record sales — but a chip shortage looms
items.content: Cook warned that Apple is facing supply-chain headwinds from RAMageddon that could impact its business.

items.title: Apple was surprised by AI-driven demand for Macs
items.content: Apple said it will be supply-constrained on Mac mini, Studio, and Neo in the next quarter, too.

items.title: Apple loses bid to pause App Store fee changes as case heads to Supreme Court
items.content: Apple lost its bid to pause court-ordered App Store payment changes, keeping external purchase links in place as its case with Epic heads toward the Supreme Court.

items.title: Paragon is not collaborating with Italian authorities probing spyware attacks, report says
items.content: Despite promising to help determine what happened with the hacks targeting journalists and activists in Italy, Israeli American spyware maker Paragon has reportedly not respon

items.title: Satya Nadella says he’s ready to ‘exploit’ the new OpenAI deal
items.content: Microsoft gets to offer OpenAI's tech to its cloud customers and doesn't have to pay for it. "We fully plan to exploit it," Nadella said.

items.title: Microsoft says it has over 20M paid Copilot users, and they really are using it
items.content: Despite the lingering perception that no one really uses Copilot, Microsoft said on Wednesday that the number of users and engagement is growing.

items.title: OpenAI ends Microsoft legal peril over its $50B Amazon deal
items.content: OpenAI has won major concessions from its largest shareholder, Microsoft, that will allow it to sell products on AWS, while Microsoft gets more cash in a revenue-share agreeme

items.title: Microsoft offers buyout for up to 7% of US employees
items.content: If a worker's years of service at Microsoft plus their age equals 70 or more, they will be eligible for a voluntary retirement buyout.

items.title: Stocks and Oil Prices Sent Conflicting Signals in April Amid Havoc of Iran War
items.content: Energy markets remained disrupted by turmoil in the Middle East, while the S&P 500 posted its best month since November 2020.

items.title: Trump’s Tariff Reversal Reopens a Major Market for Scotch Whisky
items.content: A 10 percent tax on American imports was hurting British sales, even though exporters had increased sales in India, China and other countries.

items.title: Utility Bills Are Likely to Be Higher This Summer. Here’s What You Can Do.
items.content: The average bill is forecast to rise 8.5 percent. There are simple ways to make sure you’re not overpaying, like getting your air-conditioning checked.

items.title: OpenAI’s New Model Spurs Debate Over Computing Power
items.content: Sam Altman suggested it would be released more widely than a rival offering from Anthropic. Some are suggesting it’s because OpenAI has more computing power.
